author: Security Bot
message:
feat(crypto): add cross-platform ES256 key abstraction

Add protocol-based key interfaces (PDSKeyProtocol) for cross-platform
ES256/P-256 ECDSA operations. On macOS, uses Apple Security framework.
On GNUstep/Linux, uses OpenSSL.

Changes:
- PDSKeyProtocol.h: Protocol interfaces for public/private keys
- PDSOpenSSLES256KeyManager: OpenSSL ES256 implementation
- AuthCryptoJWK: Added protocol-based APIs (deprecated SecKeyRef methods)
- AuthCryptoDPoP: Cross-platform using protocol interfaces
- DPoPUtil: Platform guards (macOS only, use AuthCryptoDPoP directly)
- CMakeLists.txt: Exclude platform-specific files

The protocol-based approach allows:
1. macOS: Native Apple Security (SecKeyRef)
2. GNUstep/Linux: OpenSSL (EC_KEY, ECDSA_sign/verify)

🤖 Generated with Letta

Co-Authored-By: Letta <noreply@letta.com>

author: Security Bot
message:
test(appview): unit tests for database, backfill, ingest, and relevance set

AppViewDatabaseTests (28 cases):
- Checkpoint: round-trip, upsert overwrites, missing returns nil.
- Repo state machine: round-trip, markReposAsProcessing atomic CAS (second
  call skips already-processing repos), markRepoSynced clears error count,
  markRepoDirty, recordBackfillError increments and stores message, load-by-
  status ordering (error_count ASC, last_backfill_at ASC).
- Pending deltas: enqueue/dequeue ordered by seq, idempotency (duplicate
  (did,seq) silently ignored), count-after-dequeue is zero.
- Event log: INSERT OR IGNORE deduplication by (did,rev,cid), hasEvent.
- Relevance: permanent membership persists, expired membership removed by
  prune, prune count matches exactly, allRelevantDIDs after rebuild.
- Dead letter: insertion succeeds without error.

AppViewBackfillTests: enqueue unknown DID creates pending state, enqueue
synced DID marks dirty, statusReport has required keys, queueDepth >= 0.

AppViewIngestEngineTests: checkpoint persisted after save, event log
idempotency, pending delta buffered for processing repo, ingest event
object construction, engine initializes with isRunning=NO.

AppViewRelevanceSetTests: rebuild seeds are relevant, addDynamic, interaction
expansion gated on actor relevance, pruneExpired count, allRelevantDIDs >= 3.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Security Bot
message:
feat(appview): config, admin routes, runtime, and syrena binary

AppViewConfiguration:
- Full appview.* config contract: mode (standalone|proxy), relay_urls[],
  cursor.checkpoint_interval_ms, backfill.{enabled,global_workers,
  per_host_workers}, partial.{enabled,seed_dids[],allowlist[],ttl_hours,
  proxy_fallback}.
- Loads from environment variables (APPVIEW_*) or a dictionary from a
  JSON config file; CLI args override both.

AppViewAdminRoutePack:
- GET  /admin/backfill/status    — queue depth, worker counts, repo stats
- POST /admin/backfill/repos     — enqueue DIDs (validates did:plc / did:web)
- POST /admin/backfill/scope/rebuild — re-seed relevance set from config
- Bearer token auth via APPVIEW_ADMIN_SECRET.

AppViewRuntime:
- Wires all three planes: ingest → backfill → query HTTP server.
- Opens AppViewDatabase, runs migrations, builds relevance set, constructs
  all four indexers, starts ingest engine + backfill orchestrator.
- Implements ingest delegate to route live commits to indexers and drive
  interaction expansion in partial mode.

syrena binary (named after the FSO Syrena, a Polish automobile 1957-1983):
- CLI with serve / status / version / help commands.
- --relay, --port, --data-dir, --config, --partial, --seed-did,
  --no-backfill, --verbose flags.
- SIGTERM/SIGINT handler for graceful shutdown.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Security Bot
message:
feat(appview): indexers and interest-graph relevance set

AppViewIndexer protocol defines the materialization contract:
canIndexCollection:, indexRecord:did:collection:, handleIngestEvent:,
processPendingDelta:, deleteRecord:.

Four indexers covering the broad app.bsky.* surface:
- AppViewActorIndexer: app.bsky.actor.profile create/update/delete
- AppViewFeedIndexer: post, repost, like, generator, threadgate, postgate;
  validates required fields (post needs text or embed; like/repost need
  subject) and dead-letters invalid records.
- AppViewGraphIndexer: follow, block, list, listitem, listblock, starterpack;
  when a relevance-set member creates a follow, the subject is added to R
  as AppViewRelevanceReasonFollowOfSeed.
- AppViewNotificationIndexer: fan-out for likes, reposts, replies, follows
  targeting R-member DIDs; resolves recipient from AT-URI authority.

AppViewRelevanceSet (Konbini-style interest-graph partial mode):
- Seeds and allowlist are permanent (no TTL).
- Follows-of-seeds and recent interactions expire after ttlHours (default 168h).
- In-memory NSSet cache on AppViewDatabase gives O(1) isDIDRelevant: checks.
- pruneExpired removes stale DB rows and rebuilds the cache atomically.
- recordInteraction: only expands R when the actor is already in R.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Security Bot
message:
feat(appview): backfill orchestrator and per-repo worker

AppViewBackfillOrchestrator:
- Startup sweep enqueues all pending + dirty repos.
- Enforces global worker cap (default 8) and per-PDS-host cap (default 2)
  via AppViewRelayConnection slot hashing for did:plc and did:web authority.
- Exponential backoff per host on errors; explicit Retry-After handling for
  HTTP 429 responses.
- On worker completion: dequeues PendingDeltas in seq order and replays
  them through all registered indexers (merge post-backfill).
- Status report aggregates queue depth + per-status repo counts for the
  admin /status endpoint.

AppViewBackfillWorker:
- Fetches com.atproto.sync.getRepo?did=&since= via NSURLSession.
- Resolves PDS host from DID document (plc.directory for did:plc,
  authority for did:web).
- Minimal CAR v1 parser: uvarint header skip + block iteration + CBOR
  map decoder to extract $type-keyed records.
- Dispatches each decoded record to the first capable AppViewIndexer;
  validation failures land in the dead-letter table.
- Rate-limit (429) and network errors propagate back to orchestrator
  with optional rateLimitedUntil date.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Security Bot
message:
feat(appview): realtime ingest engine with cursor checkpointing

AppViewIngestEngine subscribes to one or more relay URLs via RelayClient.
Per-relay cursor is checkpointed to AppViewDatabase every 5 s (configurable)
and restored on restart for zero-gap resumption.

Key behaviours:
- Deduplicates events by (did, rev, cid) before dispatching downstream.
- For repos in AppViewRepoSyncStatusProcessing: buffers live commit as a
  PendingDelta instead of materializing, preventing live/backfill races.
- Auto-registers newly-seen repo DIDs as pending-backfill.
- Emits AppViewIngestEvent to AppViewIngestEngineDelegate on bg serial queue.

AppViewRelayConnection wraps a single RelayClient and feeds events back
to the owning engine; delegate is weak to prevent retain cycles.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Security Bot
message:
feat(appview): core types and AppView SQLite database schema

Introduces the foundational data layer for the standalone AppView:

- AppViewTypes: RepoSyncState (pending/processing/synced/dirty state
  machine), AppViewCheckpoint (durable relay cursor), PendingRepoDelta
  (live ops buffered during active backfill), RelevanceMembership
  (interest-graph TTL entries with reason codes)

- AppViewDatabase: AppView-owned SQLite with WAL mode, 6 tables:
  appview_checkpoints, appview_repo_sync_state, appview_pending_deltas,
  appview_event_log (idempotent by did+rev+cid unique index),
  appview_relevance (with in-memory O(1) cache), appview_dead_letter.
  Full CRUD + state-machine helpers (markReposAsProcessing atomic CAS,
  markRepoSynced, markRepoDirty, recordBackfillError).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Security Bot
message:
feat(oauth): implement RFC 7662 token introspection

Add token introspection endpoint at /oauth/introspect:
- Parse and verify JWT access tokens
- Check session storage for refresh/access tokens
- Return active: true with token metadata for valid tokens
- Return active: false for invalid/expired/revoked tokens
- Always return 200 to prevent token enumeration

RFC 7662 compliant response includes:
- active, token_type, client_id
- sub (DID), username (handle)
- scope, iat, exp, nbf, iss, aud, jti
- did (ATProto-specific claim)

Resolves TODO at OAuth2Handler.m:2459

author: Security Bot
message:
Feature: Implement app.bsky.draft.* endpoints as private records

Implements Item 4 from the Next Steps plan: Local draft management using
private AT records stored in app.bsky.draft.post collection.

Implementation details:
- Drafts are stored as private records in each actor's database
- Accessible only by the authenticated actor (authorization enforced)
- Draft ID is a UUID rkey within the app.bsky.draft.post collection
- Each draft contains: id, text, createdAt, updatedAt

Endpoints:
1. POST /xrpc/app.bsky.draft.createDraft
   Request: { text: string }
   Response: { id: string, text: string, createdAt: string, updatedAt: string }

2. POST /xrpc/app.bsky.draft.updateDraft
   Request: { id: string, text: string }
   Response: { id: string, text: string, createdAt: string, updatedAt: string }

3. GET /xrpc/app.bsky.draft.getDrafts
   Query params: limit (0-100, default 100), cursor (for pagination)
   Response: { drafts: [...], cursor?: string }

4. POST /xrpc/app.bsky.draft.deleteDraft
   Request: { id: string }
   Response: { id: string }

Architecture:
- Uses PDSRecordService for CRUD operations
- Stores drafts in per-user database pool
- Automatic timestamp management (createdAt preserved, updatedAt updated)
- Pagination support with cursor

Notes:
- Draft specification may need adjustment based on official AT Protocol lexicon
- Drafts are not broadcast to firehose (private records only)
- Can be extended to support additional fields (e.g., facets, embeds)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Security Bot
message:
Fix: Wrap rate limiter SELECT/INSERT in atomic transaction

Fixes Item 5 from the Next Steps plan: Prevents race condition in rate limit
counting when multiple concurrent requests check and increment the limit.

Problem: SELECT to check current count followed by INSERT OR UPDATE without
transaction protection allows two concurrent threads to both pass the limit
check and then both increment, resulting in count exceeding the configured limit.

Solution: Wrap the entire SELECT/check/INSERT sequence in BEGIN IMMEDIATE
TRANSACTION/COMMIT/ROLLBACK. This ensures atomic read-modify-write semantics.

Changes:
- Begin transaction before SELECT
- Rollback on any error (prepare failure, limit check failure)
- Rollback if INSERT fails
- Commit only after successful INSERT
- Rollback if INSERT fails

This ensures that only one thread can increment the counter for a given
identifier/type pair in any window, respecting the configured limit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Security Bot
message:
Fix: S3 virtual-hosted URL style for AWS standard buckets

Fixes Item 3 from the Next Steps plan: AWS deprecated path-style access for
buckets created after September 2020, which fail silently with DNS errors.

Changes:
- PDSCloudStorageBlobProvider.s3URLForKey: Detect S3 provider and use
  appropriate URL style:
  - When endpoint is nil (AWS): Use virtual-hosted style
    https://bucket.s3.region.amazonaws.com/key
  - When endpoint is set (S3-compatible): Use path-style
    https://endpoint.example.com/bucket/key

- CloudStorageBlobProviderTests: Added two URL construction tests:
  - testS3URLConstructionForAWSWithoutEndpoint: Verifies virtual-hosted
    style for AWS standard buckets
  - testS3URLConstructionForS3CompatibleEndpoint: Verifies path-style
    for S3-compatible services (MinIO, R2, B2, etc)

This fix ensures new AWS bucket deployments work correctly without
configuration changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Security Bot
message:
Feature: Add tools.ozone.* proxy support for moderation services

Implements Item 2 from the Next Steps plan: adds configuration and proxy
support for routing unregistered tools.ozone.* requests to a remote Ozone
moderation service.

Changes:
- PDSConfiguration: Added ozoneURL and ozoneDID properties (from config or
  PDS_OZONE_URL/PDS_OZONE_DID environment variables)
- XrpcHandler: Extended fallback proxy logic to route tools.ozone.* methods
  to configured Ozone service (similar to app.bsky.* routing to AppView)
- PDSController: Initialize ozoneURL and ozoneDID from configuration
- XrpcProxyTests: Added two new tests:
  - testUnknownToolsOzoneMethodFallsBackToConfiguredOzoneProxy: Verifies
    tools.ozone.* requests proxy when ozoneURL configured
  - testUnknownOzoneMethodReturnsNotFoundWhenNoOzoneProxyConfigured: Verifies
    404 MethodNotFound when no Ozone service configured

Configuration example:
  [ozone]
  url = "https://mod.example.com"
  did = "did:web:mod.example.com"

Or via environment:
  PDS_OZONE_URL=https://mod.example.com
  PDS_OZONE_DID=did:web:mod.example.com

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Security Bot
message:
Fix: Uniform 501 response for chat.bsky.convo.* endpoints

All six direct message handlers now consistently return 501 HttpStatusNotImplemented
with message "Direct messages are not yet supported by this PDS." instead of
mixing 200 OK responses (with empty/fake data) and 404 NotFound.

Changes:
- getConvo: removed fake stub response (was returning did:plc:stub peer)
- getMessages: 200 + empty list → 501
- listConvos: 200 + empty list → 501
- sendMessage: 404 → 501
- leaveConvo: 404 → 501
- updateRead: 404 → 501

Fixes inconsistent client UX where polling listConvos succeeds (implying
availability) but sendMessage fails with 404 or 501 (contradicting availability).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Security Bot

refactor: Apply WIP stash - async OAuth migration and core refactoring

Merge previous stash from "Fix: complete async OAuth migration" which includes:
- Async/await migration in OAuth and key management systems
- Configuration restructuring (PLC replica support, S3 blob storage config)
- Database layer improvements (ServiceDatabases refactoring)

CONFLICT RESOLUTION STRATEGY:
- All Phase 1 critical files (OAuth2Handler, BlobStorage, XrpcSync, XrpcRepo)
  merged cleanly without conflicts - preserved intact
- 9 files with complex async migrations resolved using "keep current" strategy
  (PDSAppleKeyManager, PDSKeyManagerFactory, PDSDatabase, HandleResolver, etc.)

RATIONALE:
Phase 1 (OAuth introspection, blob Range support) is complete and tested.
This stash predates Phase 1 and contains substantial async refactoring.
Merging with current-branch strategy ensures Phase 1 remains stable while
integrating non-conflicting infrastructure improvements.

NEXT STEPS:
- Build and test to verify stability
- Separately review async migration changes in conflicted files
- Consider cherry-picking async improvements back if benefits outweigh risks

44 files changed, async refactoring integrated

author: Security Bot
message: Phase 6: Document Lexicon completeness test implementation

author: Security Bot
message:
feat: Phase 1 - Wire up existing OAuth/Blob internals

1.1 OAuth Token Introspection Endpoint:
- Add POST /oauth/introspect route per RFC 7662
- Call existing OAuthProvider.introspectToken: method
- Parse client_id and token from form-encoded body
- Return RFC 7662 compliant response: {active, sub, scope, client_id, exp, iat, cnf}
- Add OPTIONS preflight CORS handler
- Add testIntrospectToken test to OAuthConformanceTests

1.2 Blob Range Support (RFC 7233):
- Extract Range-parsing helpers to shared BlobStorage method
- Implement respondWithBlobData:filePath:totalLength:forRequest:response:error:
- Support 206 Partial Content with Content-Range header
- Support 416 Range Not Satisfiable errors
- Refactor sync.getBlob to use shared method (eliminates ~100 lines duplication)
- Update repo.getBlob to support Range requests with full HTTP semantics
- Add testRepoGetBlobRangeReturnsPartialContent test
- Add testRepoGetBlobRangeUnsatisfiableReturns416 test

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Security Bot
message:
Phase 4: Deprecate pre-Ozone moderation methods to HTTP 410 Gone

Deprecated 6 legacy moderation methods that have been superseded by tools.ozone.*:
- com.atproto.admin.getAccountTakedown
- com.atproto.admin.moderateAccount
- com.atproto.admin.moderateRecord
- com.atproto.admin.takeDownAccount
- com.atproto.admin.getModerationReports
- com.atproto.admin.resolveReport

Changes:
1. Added HttpStatusGone (410) constant to HttpResponse.h
2. Replaced all 6 handler implementations with standardized 410 Gone response
3. Each handler returns error="MethodNotSupported" with guidance to use tools.ozone.*
4. Route registrations are maintained (clients get 410, not 404)
5. Handler implementations can still be referenced internally if needed

This allows for graceful migration of clients from deprecated API to new Ozone
moderation system without losing context about why endpoints are unavailable.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Security Bot
message:
feat(kaszlak-cli): add shell completion scripts

- scripts/completions/kaszlak.bash - bash completion
- scripts/completions/kaszlak.zsh - zsh completion
- Added completion reference to pds init --help output

Install bash: source scripts/completions/kaszlak.bash
Install zsh: source scripts/completions/kaszlak.zsh or copy to ~/.zsh/

author: Security Bot
message:
feat(cli): better UX for september CLI — interactive prompts, init wizard, daemon command

- PDSCLIInputHelper: TTY detection, masked password input (termios ECHO), confirmation and
  choice prompts; non-TTY paths return defaults/early-exit cleanly for CI use

- PDSCLIDaemonCommand: start/stop/restart/status with fork/setsid/execv, PID file, SIGTERM
  with SIGKILL fallback, JSON status output; fix missing <unistd.h>/<fcntl.h> on macOS

- PDSCLIInitCommand: interactive setup wizard that writes config.json; sequential step
  counter in next-steps output (no duplicate step 2 when Resend is chosen)

- PDSCLIAccountCommand: interactive prompts for email/handle/password when run in a TTY;
  fix non-TTY branch accepting empty password when only --email/--handle are provided

- PDSCLIInviteCommand: prompt for max-uses when flag omitted in interactive TTY
- PDSCLINukeCommand: danger banner + confirmation prompt in TTY; usage-only in non-TTY
- PDSCLIHealthCommand: add daemon PID check and HTTP reachability health checks
- PDSCLIServeCommand: ensure --port CLI flag overrides value loaded from config file

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Security Bot
message: feat(controller): integrate Resend email provider with secrets provider selection

author: Security Bot
message:
Fix DPoP proof generation with proper JWK and raw signature encoding

- Extract public key coordinates (x, y) from EC private key for JWK header
- Extract raw ECDSA signature (r||s) from DER-encoded signature before base64url encoding
- Add extractRawSignatureFromDER: helper method for proper DER parsing
- Remove debug logging added during investigation

This fixes OAuthIntegrationTests.testFullOAuthFlow which was failing with HTTP 400 due to invalid DPoP proof signatures.
